description:
You are acting as the “configuration-manager” for a small company.  
    Your job today is to create a reproducible, verifiable **daily backup** of
    the directory  
        /home/user/projects/configs  
    and to log meta-data about that backup so that an automated system can later
    confirm its integrity.

    Starting situation
    ------------------
    1. The directory /home/user/projects/configs already exists and contains exactly
       these four files (nothing else):
         • httpd.conf  
         • sshd.conf  
         • database.conf  
         • app.conf  

       Each file is plain text and already filled with configuration data.  
       Do NOT modify their contents.

    2. The directory /home/user/backups already exists and is writable by the current
       user.

    Task
    ----
    Perform the following steps **using 5–10 individual shell commands**:

    1. Create a **gzip-compressed tar archive** that contains the whole directory
       /home/user/projects/configs.  
       • Name of the archive:
             /home/user/backups/YYYYMMDD_configs_backup.tar.gz  
         where YYYYMMDD must be replaced by today’s calendar date in the
         system’s local time zone (e.g. 20240517_configs_backup.tar.gz).  
       • The archive must preserve the directory structure, i.e. when it is
         extracted it must re-create the folder “configs” with the four *.conf
         files inside it.

    2. Count how many “*.conf” files are present **inside the original source
       directory** (not inside the archive) and remember that number.

    3. Generate a SHA-256 checksum of the archive you just created.

    4. Append **one single line** to the log file
           /home/user/backups/backup_manifest.log  
       using exactly the following key-value format **in one line, no spaces
       around “=” signs, fields separated by a single comma**:

           DATE=YYYY-MM-DD,ARCHIVE=/home/user/backups/YYYYMMDD_configs_backup.tar.gz,SHA256=<checksum>,FILECOUNT=<number>

       Rules for the log line:
       • DATE must be written in ISO form (YYYY-MM-DD, same local date you used
         in the filename).  
       • ARCHIVE must be the full absolute path to the *.tar.gz file.  
       • SHA256 must be the 64-character hex string produced by the sha256sum
         command (lower-case).  
       • FILECOUNT must be the decimal number of “*.conf” files you counted in
         step 2.  
       • The line must **end with a single newline character** (ASCII 0x0A).

       Example (formatted for readability – your actual SHA256 will be
       different and there must be no line breaks):

           DATE=2024-05-17,ARCHIVE=/home/user/backups/20240517_configs_backup.tar.gz,SHA256=4d0123f…c9e,FILECOUNT=4⏎

       (The “⏎” symbol merely indicates the trailing newline.)

    What the automated checker will look for
    ----------------------------------------
    • /home/user/backups must contain exactly one file that matches the pattern
      [0-9]\{8\}_configs_backup.tar.gz and its modification date must be today.  
    • Extracting that archive must produce a top-level directory named “configs”
      that in turn contains exactly the four original *.conf files.  
    • /home/user/backups/backup_manifest.log must exist and its **last**
      line must match the key-value format specified above.  
    • DATE must equal today’s date, ARCHIVE must equal the absolute archive
      path, SHA256 must match the checksum of the archive, and FILECOUNT must be “4”.
    • The agent should accomplish all of this in no fewer than 5 and no more
      than 10 individual shell commands.

    Important notes
    ---------------
    • Do not move or delete the original /home/user/projects/configs directory.  
    • The log file might already exist from previous days; append your new
      entry at the end, do not overwrite old ones.  
    • Work as the normal “user”; no root or sudo commands are required.
truth:
Initial state supplied by the test harness
    -----------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/projects/configs (mode 0755) with four files:
       a) /home/user/projects/configs/httpd.conf
          Content (exact):
              # Apache HTTPD configuration
              ServerName localhost
              Listen 80

       b) /home/user/projects/configs/sshd.conf
          Content:
              # OpenSSH daemon configuration
              Port 22
              PermitRootLogin no

       c) /home/user/projects/configs/database.conf
          Content:
              # Example DB configuration
              host=127.0.0.1
              port=5432

       d) /home/user/projects/configs/app.conf
          Content:
              # Application configuration
              debug=true
              workers=4

       All four files have mtime “2020-01-01 00:00:00 UTC” so that archive
       creation is deterministic if the agent uses “--sort=name
       --mtime='2020-01-01'” (not mandatory but allowed).

    2. Directory /home/user/backups (mode 0755) is present and empty except,
       possibly, for an older backup_manifest.log created on earlier test runs.

    3. The automated checker, after the agent finishes, will compute:
       • TODAY      → the current calendar date in the container’s local TZ.
       • TODAY_RAW  → the same date in YYYYMMDD form.
       • ARCHIVE    → "/home/user/backups/${TODAY_RAW}_configs_backup.tar.gz"
       • FILECOUNT  → 4
       • SHA256     → `sha256sum "${ARCHIVE}" | awk '{print $1}'`

       It will then read the final line of /home/user/backups/backup_manifest.log
       and verify that it equals exactly:
           "DATE=${TODAY},ARCHIVE=${ARCHIVE},SHA256=${SHA256},FILECOUNT=4\n"

       (No leading/trailing spaces, commas exactly as above.)

    4. Permissions: All files/directories are owned by user “user” (UID 1000,
       GID 1000) and are writable/readable.

    5. No other files beginning with digits and ending in
       _configs_backup.tar.gz are present in /home/user/backups before the
       agent starts.
